dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used price_pos_20 (20-day price position) to identify new 20-day highs (>=0.9) and lows (<=0.1). Stop loss placed at opposite extreme using price_pos_20 thresholds. Hold period limited to 2 days per signal requirement to capture early breakout and avoid crowding slippage. Risk-reward ratio of 1.5:1 encoded in stop_loss_pct as a proportional rule.

backtest_rule.reason: Used price_pos_20 (20-day price position) to identify new 20-day highs (>=0.9) and lows (<=0.1). Stop loss placed at opposite extreme using price_pos_20 thresholds. Hold period limited to 2 days per signal requirement to capture early breakout and avoid crowding slippage. Risk-reward ratio of 1.5:1 encoded in stop_loss_pct as a proportional rule.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter breakout positions in the direction of new price extremes, but should be aware that obvious breakout setups become crowded and are subject to fakeouts; therefore, improve entry odds by either trading the breakout itself or contrarian entries that exploit the predictable failure of obvious breakouts.
